items.front: 9-7-1: When to coordinate parachute jump information
items.back: Prior to AND at the end of each jump or series of jumps that begins or ends in your area of jurisdiction. Coordinate with other affected ATC facilities/sectors. 14 CFR 105.15 prescribes the required information.

items.front: 9-7-2: Three requirements for parachute ops in Class A, B, C airspace
items.back: 1) Authorize only within designated jump airspace. 2) Separate nonparticipating aircraft from that airspace. 3) Impose any conditions/restrictions to promote safety. Ref: 14 CFR 105.25.
For Class D with functioning tower: 1) Authorize with respect to known or observed traffic. 2) Issue advisories to jump aircraft AND nonparticipating aircraft as necessary.

items.front: 9-7-3 vs 9-7-2: Class D vs Class A/B/C parachute handling
items.back: Class A/B/C (9-7-2): designate airspace + separate nonparticipants. Class D (9-7-3): authorize wrt known/observed traffic + advisories. Class D standard is less restrictive.
Aircraft type, altitude, and direction of flight of all KNOWN traffic that will transit the jump airspace. Issued before the jump.
Location, time, duration, and altitude from which the jump will be made.

items.front: 9-7-4: Alternative broadcast methods for jump advisories (Class E)
items.back: When individual transmissions impractical: broadcast on appropriate control frequencies or ATIS (when available).

items.front: 9-7-4: 14 CFR 105.13 radio requirement for Class E jumps
items.back: Must establish radio contact at least 5 minutes before jumping begins to receive traffic info, unless otherwise authorized by ATC.
